text.title: Distribution First Ideation Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Distribution First Ideation application MUST enforce, plus a terminal skip-this-methodology rule.

rule.statement: Decision-record MUST name the distribution channel (newsletter URL, Reddit subreddit, Twitter handle, etc.) with current reach metric. 'Word of mouth' fails the test.
rule.rationale: Distribution-first research — anchors Distribution First Ideation rule `named-channel-required`.

rule.statement: Smoke page MUST hit a named traffic target within 14 days (default: ≥500 visitors). Missing the target = channel does not have the implied reach for this idea.
rule.rationale: Smoke-page validation — anchors Distribution First Ideation rule `smoke-page-traffic-target`.

rule.statement: Waitlist conversion MUST be ≥10% of smoke-page traffic. Below = audience interest is not strong enough; route to a different idea.
rule.rationale: Conversion-rate research — anchors Distribution First Ideation rule `waitlist-conversion-floor`.

rule.statement: If smoke-page traffic OR waitlist conversion misses target, the idea MUST be killed (or paused with revisit date). Sunk-cost continuation rejected.
rule.rationale: Falsifiability discipline — anchors Distribution First Ideation rule `kill-on-negative-signal`.

rule.statement: Output is a single decision-record per idea with channel + smoke URL + traffic + conversion + verdict. Verbal-only decisions forbidden.
rule.rationale: Decision-record practice — anchors Distribution First Ideation rule `decision-record-go-no-go`.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition or triggered skip, then route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
